title: CLAUDE.md
text: Never attempt run bash commands Never try run git commands Never try search outside workspace Never duplicate code unless ask approval get consent Never search code get answer. Always ask help insteaad. Never guess method implementations. API documentation authoritative complete. docs list attribute/parameter one entity another, distinction intentional — follow literally. Never ask confirm something documentation already states unambiguously. Ask , act less implementation genuinely undetermined — never re-ask docs prior answers already settle. may follow existing patterns codebase, verify pattern correct reusing . Existing code automatically correct. implementing new function, review files starting direct-, job-, utils- R/ directory.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: repository R package arcgisrouting part R-ArcGIS Bridge. provides bindings ArcGIS Routing REST API documented https://developers.arcgis.com/rest/routing/. uses arcgisutils Esri JSON handling including date conversions geoprocessing parameters. use strong type checking via standalone type checks defined R/import-standalone-types-check.R always use rlang low level actions Errors warnings created using cli Wrting duplicate functions duplicate vectors banned validating type object always create reusable function. Comments shuold never describe function call . sapply() banned always use stronger typed apply() lapply() Always use compact() remove NULL elements list Always use #' @inheritParams documentation whenever possible. Never duplicate documentation manually Never try align arguments. Never add unecessary whitepace stylistic reasons. Never modify code outside file currently implemented. spot duplicate improvement elsewhere, mention user move . touch .

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Naming: snake_case R names mapping API names internally. Reuse name whenever concept across functions. Ordering: required spatial inputs first, core behavioral params, optional feature inputs, output control params, token always last. Defaults: NULL anything optional — dropped via compact(). Non-NULL defaults omitting surprise user. Validation: every validated param gets reusable function, never inline. Shared validators go utils-*.R, function-specific stay file. Pattern: NULL early return → scalar type check → arg_match → lookup vector API value. Never duplicate validator already exists. Spatial vs tabular inputs: use sf/sfc whenever geometry involved. Use plain data.frame attribute-tables. get dedicated as_*() converter using UseMethod dispatch — recognizing snake_case column names, validating types, renaming API names, serializing via arcgisutils::as_esri_featureset(). Type-converter functions (as_*()) STRICTLY INTERNAL. Never mention documentation, @param descriptions, examples, @seealso user-facing endpoint functions. called internally endpoint functions; instructing users call (e.g. “Use as_stops() prepare stops”) produces broken code, endpoint already runs conversion. Document raw sf/sfc/data.frame input recognized snake_case columns directly instead.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: Never write result parser seeing actual results. workflow : Implement job constructor without result_fn — default RcppSimdJson::fparse() runs returns data frame. Ask user run job share result$paramName result$dataType (vectors, full data frame). GPFeatureRecordSetLayer → try_parse(json, "/N/value") GPRecordSet → try_parse(json, "/N/value") GPBoolean / GPString → RcppSimdJson::fparse(json, query = "/N/value") GPDataFile → RcppSimdJson::fparse(json, query = "/N/value") — return file URLs, include , skip raw results JSON 0-indexed array — use numeric JSON Pointer paths ("/0/value", "/1/value", etc.) name-based paths. Write parse_<endpoint>_results(json) returning compact() named list snake_case keys derived paramName. Pass function result_fn positional argument (third arg) $new() call. token must passed named argument.

title: ArcGIS Routing for R
text: arcgisrouting brings ArcGIS Routing REST API R. Plan routes, measure travel time distance, build service areas, route fleets vehicles, snap GPS tracks roads, returning sf objects ready analysis mapping. part R-ArcGIS Bridge. [!IMPORTANT] Using arcgisrouting requires ArcGIS Online account, ArcGIS Location Platform account, ArcGIS Enterprise server. Routing operations consume ArcGIS credits. pricing, see ArcGIS Routing pricing details.

title: ArcGIS Routing for R
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("r-arcgis/arcgisrouting")

title: ArcGIS Routing for R
text: Every request authorized ArcGIS token. Authenticate per session arcgisutils package pick token automatically. See R-ArcGIS Bridge authentication guide client credentials, API keys, workflows.
code: library(arcgisutils) library(arcgisrouting)  set_arc_token(auth_user())

title: ArcGIS Routing for R
text: Find best route ordered set stops:
code: library(sf) library(arcgisutils) library(arcgisrouting)  set_arc_token(auth_user())  stops <- st_sf(   name = c("Ferry Building", "Coit Tower", "Painted Ladies"),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(-122.3933, 37.7955)),     st_point(c(-122.4058, 37.8024)),     st_point(c(-122.4329, 37.7762)),     crs = 4326   ) )  route <- find_routes(stops) route$routes

title: ArcGIS Routing for R
text: direct request functions return immediately best interactive work modest inputs. asynchronous geoprocessing job functions queue job server, ideal large problems. Working job looks like : Discover travel modes available organization get_travel_modes().
code: job <- find_routes_job(stops)  job$start()      # submit the job to the server job$await()      # poll until the job completes job$messages()   # retrieve the geoprocessing job messages job$results      # read the results once the job has finished

code: find_closest_facilities(   incidents,   facilities,   travel_mode = NULL,   default_target_facility_count = NULL,   travel_direction = NULL,   default_cutoff = NULL,   time_of_day = NULL,   time_of_day_usage = NULL,   u_turns = NULL,   use_hierarchy = NULL,   impedance = NULL,   accumulate_impedance = NULL,   restrictions = NULL,   attribute_parameter_values = NULL,   point_barriers = NULL,   line_barriers = NULL,   polygon_barriers = NULL,   return_geometry = c("facilities", "incidents"),   directions_language = NULL,   directions_output_type = NULL,   directions_style = NULL,   directions_length_units = NULL,   directions_time_attribute = NULL,   output_lines = "true_shape",   ignore_invalid_locations = TRUE,   preserve_object_id = FALSE,   return_empty_results = FALSE,   output_geometry_precision = 10,   output_geometry_precision_units = "meters",   geometry_precision = NULL,   geometry_precision_m = NULL,   locate_settings = NULL,   crs = 4326,   token = arcgisutils::arc_token() )

title: Find Closest Facilities — find_closest_facilities
text: incidents sf sfc object containing point geometries representing locations search . facilities sf sfc object containing point geometries representing facilities search . travel_mode Character. name travel mode use. See get_travel_modes() available options. Default: NULL. default_target_facility_count default NULL. integer scalar. number closest facilities find per incident. travel_direction default "away". scalar character. One "away" (away facility) "towards" (toward facility). default_cutoff default NULL. numeric scalar. travel time distance value stop searching facilities. time_of_day_usage default NULL. scalar character. One "start_time" "end_time". Specifies whether time_of_day represents departure arrival time. use_hierarchy Logical. Whether use hierarchy finding routes. Default: NULL. restrictions Character vector. Restriction names honor. Default: NULL. attribute_parameter_values default NULL. list objects. Additional values required attribute restriction. polygon_barriers Polygon barriers sf sfc object. Default: NULL. return_geometry default c("facilities", "incidents"). character vector. Valid values: "cf_routes", "facilities", "incidents", "directions", "barriers", "polyline_barriers", "polygon_barriers", "traversed_edges", "traversed_junctions", "traversed_turns". Use "everything" return . directions_language Character. Language code directions (e.g., "en"). Default: "en". directions_output_type default NULL. scalar character. One "standard", "complete", "complete_no_events", "instructions_only", "summary_only", "feature_sets". directions_style default NULL. scalar character. One "desktop", "navigation", "campus". directions_length_units default NULL. scalar character. One "miles", "kilometers", "feet", "meters", "yards", "nautical_miles". directions_time_attribute default NULL. scalar character. time-based impedance attribute used direction durations. output_lines default NULL (lines). scalar character NULL. One "true_shape" "with_measure". ignore_invalid_locations Logical. Whether ignore invalid locations. Default: TRUE. preserve_object_id default FALSE. logical scalar. Preserves object IDs input locations output. return_empty_results default FALSE. logical scalar. Returns empty results instead error failure. output_geometry_precision default 10. numeric scalar. Simplification tolerance applied output geometry. output_geometry_precision_units default "meters". scalar character. Units output_geometry_precision. valid values trim_polygon_distance_units. geometry_precision default NULL. integer scalar. Decimal places x y values response geometries. geometry_precision_m default NULL. scalar character. Decimal places m-values response geometries. locate_settings default NULL. list controlling inputs located network. crs default 4326. coordinate reference system output geometries. Passed arcgisutils::as_spatial_reference(). token Authorization token. Default: arcgisutils::arc_token().

text: list containing routing resps. elements returned depend return_geometry parameter. Use decode_compressed_geometry() decode sf geometries. stops: Stop features barriers: Barrier features polyline_barriers: Polyline barrier features polygon_barriers: Polygon barrier features traversed_edges: Traversed edge features traversed_junctions: Traversed junction features traversed_turns: Traversed turn features messages: Status warning messages service directions_type = "feature_sets", response includes: direction_points: sf object point features direction maneuvers direction_lines: sf object line features route segments

title: Find Routes — find_routes
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(sf) library(arcgisutils) set_arc_token(auth_user())  # Simple route between 3 stops stops <- st_sf(   name = c("Start", "Middle", "End"),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(-122.4194, 37.7749)),  # San Francisco     st_point(c(-122.0312, 37.3318)),  # Cupertino     st_point(c(-121.8863, 37.3382)),  # San Jose     crs = 4326   ) )  resp <- find_routes(stops)  # Route with time windows for deliveries delivery_stops <- st_sf(   name = c("Warehouse", "Customer A", "Customer B", "Customer C"),   time_window_start = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-01-15 08:00:00",     "2024-01-15 09:00:00",     "2024-01-15 11:00:00",     "2024-01-15 14:00:00"   )),   time_window_end = as.POSIXct(c(     "2024-01-15 08:30:00",     "2024-01-15 10:00:00",     "2024-01-15 13:00:00",     "2024-01-15 16:00:00"   )),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(-122.4194, 37.7749)),     st_point(c(-122.4083, 37.7858)),     st_point(c(-122.4313, 37.7793)),     st_point(c(-122.4000, 37.7900)),     crs = 4326   ) )  delivery_route <- find_routes(   delivery_stops,   start_time = as.POSIXct("2024-01-15 08:00:00"),   travel_mode = "Driving Time" )  # Find optimal order for stops sales_stops <- st_sf(   name = c("Office", "Client 1", "Client 2", "Client 3", "Office"),   geometry = st_sfc(     st_point(c(-122.4194, 37.7749)),     st_point(c(-122.4083, 37.7858)),     st_point(c(-122.4313, 37.7793)),     st_point(c(-122.4000, 37.7900)),     st_point(c(-122.4194, 37.7749)),     crs = 4326   ) )  optimized_route <- find_routes(   sales_stops,   find_best_sequence = TRUE,   preserve_first_stop = TRUE,   preserve_last_stop = TRUE )  # Route with barriers barrier_pts <- st_sfc(   st_point(c(-122.4150, 37.7800)),   crs = 4326 )  route_with_barriers <- find_routes(   stops,   barriers = barrier_pts )  # Accumulate multiple impedances multi_impedance_route <- find_routes(   stops,   impedance_attribute_name = "travel_time",   accumulate_attribute_names = c("miles", "kilometers") ) } # }

Default: NULL. order_pairs data.frame pairing pickup delivery orders. Default: NULL. excess_transit_factor Character. Importance reducing excess transit time order pairs. One : "low", "medium", "high". Default: NULL (API default: "medium"). uturn_policy Character. U-turn policy junctions. One : "allow_uturn", "allow_dead_ends_and_intersections_only", "allow_dead_ends_only", "no_uturns". Default: NULL. restrictions Character vector. Restriction names apply. Default: NULL. impedance Character. Impedance type. One : "travel_time", "minutes", "truck_travel_time", "truck_minutes", "walk_time", "miles", "kilometers". Default: NULL. time_impedance Character. Time-based impedance. One : "minutes", "travel_time", "walk_time", "truck_minutes", "truck_travel_time". Default: NULL. distance_impedance Character. Distance-based impedance. One : "miles", "kilometers". Default: NULL. use_hierarchy_in_analysis Logical. Whether use street network hierarchy finding routes. Default: TRUE. populate_route_lines Logical. Whether output routes include exact street shape. Default: TRUE. route_line_simplification_tolerance Numeric. Simplification tolerance route geometry. Default: NULL. populate_directions Logical. Whether generate driving directions route. Default: FALSE. directions_language Character. Language code directions (e.g. "en"). Default: NULL. directions_style_name Character. Formatting style directions. One : "desktop", "navigation", "campus". Default: NULL. save_route_data Logical. Whether save results .zip file geodatabase. Default: FALSE. save_output_layer Logical. Whether save analysis network analysis layer package. Default: FALSE. populate_stop_shapes Logical. Whether output stops include point geometries. Default: FALSE. output_format Character. Format output features. One : "feature_set", "json_file", "geojson_file". Default: "feature_set". ignore_invalid_order_locations Logical. Whether ignore invalid orders instead failing. Default: FALSE. point_barriers Point barriers sf sfc object. Default: NULL. line_barriers Line barriers sf sfc object. Default: NULL. polygon_barriers Polygon barriers sf sfc object. Default: NULL. token Authorization token. Default: arcgisutils::arc_token().

title: Snap Points to Roads — snap_to_roads
text: Snaps set GPS points likely roads traveled optionally returns traversed road segments lines using ArcGIS /SnapToRoads service.

title: Snap Points to Roads — snap_to_roads
code: snap_to_roads(   points,   travel_mode = NULL,   point_properties = c("oid_routing_streets", "posted_speed_limit_kph",     "posted_speed_limit_mph", "posted_speed_limit_mps", "posted_truck_speed_limit_kph",     "posted_truck_speed_limit_mph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mps"),   line_properties = c("oid_routing_streets", "length_kilometers", "length_miles",     "posted_speed_limit_kph", "posted_speed_limit_mph", "posted_speed_limit_mps",     "posted_truck_speed_limit_kph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mph",     "posted_truck_speed_limit_mps"),   analysis_region = c("europe", "japan", "korea", "middleastandafrica", "northamerica",     "southamerica", "southasia", "thailand"),   return_lines = TRUE,   token = arc_token() )

title: Snap Points to Roads — snap_to_roads
text: points sf sfc object containing point geometries snap road network. Recognized attribute columns track_id location_timestamp used present. travel_mode Character. name travel mode use. See get_travel_modes() available options. Default: NULL. point_properties Character vector. Road properties returned snapped points output. One : "oid_routing_streets", "posted_speed_limit_kph", "posted_speed_limit_mph", "posted_speed_limit_mps", "posted_truck_speed_limit_kph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mps". line_properties Character vector. Road properties returned output lines. One : "oid_routing_streets", "length_kilometers", "length_miles", "posted_speed_limit_kph", "posted_speed_limit_mph", "posted_speed_limit_mps", "posted_truck_speed_limit_kph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mph", "posted_truck_speed_limit_mps". Ignored unless return_lines = TRUE. analysis_region Character. Region perform analysis. One : "europe", "japan", "korea", "middleastandafrica", "northamerica", "southamerica", "southasia", "thailand". Default: auto-detected location points. return_lines Logical. Whether return output lines representing roads traversed. Default: TRUE. token Authorization token. Default: arcgisutils::arc_token().

text: named list: snapped_points: input points snapped road network snap_lines: traversed road segments (return_lines = TRUE) usage_cost: list numObjects credits used messages: status warning messages service
